toc_content:
Explanation of Application Interfaces of the 
Powertrain Domain  
AUTOSAR CP Release 4.4.0    
5 of 34 Document ID 269: AUTOSAR_EXP_AIPowertrain  
  - AUTOS AR confidential - 
Table of Contents  
1 Purpose of this Document  :  :  :   6 
2 References  :  :  :  :   7 
3 Description of Terms and Concepts  :  :  .... 8 
3.1 Abbreviation s :  :  :   8 
3.2 Terminology – Torque within the Powertrain Domain  :   8 
3.3 Terminology – Fast and Slow Torque Requests  :  :  9 
3.4 Typical combinations of signals for transmission shift intervention  :   11 
3.5 Overview of AUTOSAR torque application interfaces  :   13 
4 Architecture Overview  :  :  :   19 
5 Description of Exemplary Software Components  :  :   21 
5.1 Powertrain Coordinator – PTC (PtCoorr)  :  :   21 
5.2 Transmission System (Trsm)  :  :  .. 21 
5.3 Combustion Engin e (CmbEng)  :  :   22 
5.3.1  Engine Speed and Position (EngSpdAndPosn)  :  .. 22 
5.3.2  Engine Torque Mode Management (EngTqModMngt)  :   23 
5.3.3  Combustion Engine: Miscellaneous (CmbEngMisc)  :   23 
5.3.4  Combustion Engine: Timing - and Accuracy requirements to the signal 
“Engine Speed Including Start Stop”, necessary for transmission.  :   24 
5.4 Vehicle Motion relevant for Powertrain (VehMtnForPt)  :   26 
5.4.1  Driver Request (DrvReq)  :  :  :  26 
5.4.2  Accelerator Pedal Position (AccrPedlPosn)  :  :   27 
5.4.3 Safety Vehicle Speed Limitation (VehSpdLimnForSfty)  :   27 
5.4.4  Vehicle Motion (Powertrain): Miscellaneous (VehMtnForPtMisc)  :   27 
5.5 Powertrain: Miscellaneous (PtMisc)  :  :   27 
6 Additional Information  :  :  :   28 
6.1 Differences between SW -Cs and ECUs  :  :   28 
6.2 Function al safety  :  :  :   28 
6.3 Powertrain Application Interfaces - Decisions / Assumptions  :   28 
6.3.1  Scope  :  :  :  .. 28 
6.3.2  PTC Composition (PtCoorr)  :  :   28 
6.3.3  Definition of overboost  :  :  :   28 
6.3.4  Coordination at the vehicle level  :  :   29 
6.3.5  PTC Arbitration between Driver and Chassis torque requests  :   29 
6.3.6  Assumptions on modeling style and naming aspects specific for 
powe rtrain domain  :  :  :   30
